content:
Hilton Head Island isn't trying to be the Hamptons. It isn't trying to be Palm Beach. Over five decades of careful development, this 12-mile barrier island has created something entirely its own — a luxury lifestyle that values nature as much as refinement.

Design by Nature

Unlike most resort communities, Hilton Head was developed with strict covenants that keep buildings below the tree line and signage to a minimum. The result is an island where you're more likely to see dolphins than billboards, where canopied roads replace strip malls, and where the natural landscape is the primary amenity.

World-Class Golf

With over 30 championship courses, Hilton Head is one of the top golf destinations in the world. Harbour Town Golf Links, home of the annual RBC Heritage tournament, is just the beginning.

The Real Estate

From oceanfront estates in Sea Pines to modern villas overlooking the marsh, Hilton Head's real estate market reflects the island's unique character. Properties here aren't just homes — they're lifetime investments in a lifestyle that gets better with every visit.

A Community, Not Just a Resort

What truly sets Hilton Head apart is that it's a real community. Year-round residents, thriving local businesses, excellent dining, and a cultural scene that includes everything from symphony concerts to outdoor art festivals. This isn't a place you visit — it's a place you belong.
excerpt: How a barrier island became one of America's most coveted luxury destinations.
title: Luxury Living: What Makes Hilton Head Different

content:
Forget the mall. Hilton Head's shopping scene is about discovery — one-of-a-kind boutiques, local artisans, and curated collections you won't find replicated in every beach town on the coast.

Coligny Plaza

The heart of the island's retail scene. Over 60 shops and restaurants in an open-air setting steps from the beach. Highlights include the Jean-Pierre Klifa boutique for resort fashion, plus local galleries, surf shops, and specialty stores.

Harbour Town

The shops at Harbour Town in Sea Pines offer a more curated experience. Art galleries, jewelry designers, and upscale clothing stores line the marina walkway. Don't miss the view from the lighthouse.

The Village at Wexford

A quieter, more refined shopping experience. Wexford's courtyard setting houses high-end boutiques, wine shops, and some of the island's best dining. It's where locals go when they want to avoid the tourist crowds.

Shelter Cove

Shelter Cove Town Centre has evolved into a modern retail hub with both national brands and local favorites. The adjacent marina adds waterfront ambiance to every shopping trip.
excerpt: From Coligny Plaza to Harbour Town, the island's best boutiques and galleries.
title: Shopping Coligny & Beyond: A Boutique Guide

content:
Hilton Head Island is surrounded by some of the most beautiful waterways on the Atlantic coast. The Intracoastal Waterway, Calibogue Sound, and the open Atlantic create a playground that's as varied as it is stunning.

Dolphin Watching

Hilton Head is home to one of the largest populations of Atlantic bottlenose dolphins on the East Coast. Dozens of charter operations offer dolphin watching tours, but the best experiences come from smaller boats that can navigate the shallow creeks where dolphins strand-feed — a behavior unique to this stretch of coastline.

Fishing Charters

Whether you're after redfish in the marshes or cobia offshore, Hilton Head's fishing is world-class. Inshore charters explore the labyrinth of tidal creeks, while deep-sea trips target everything from mahi-mahi to marlin. Spring and fall are prime seasons.

Kayaking & Paddleboarding

For a quieter water experience, guided kayak tours through the island's salt marshes reveal a hidden world of herons, egrets, and the occasional dolphin. Dawn paddles are especially magical.

Sunset Sails

Nothing caps a day on Hilton Head like a sunset sail from Harbour Town. As the lighthouse silhouettes against orange sky and dolphins play in the wake, you understand why people keep coming back to this island.
excerpt: Dolphin cruises, deep-sea charters, and sunset sails — the island's best experiences happen on the water.
title: Boating & Water Adventures on Hilton Head Island

content:
Before there was a bright little boutique in Coligny Plaza, there was a boy in Paris who grew up under the cutting tables of his mother's couture atelier. That boy was Jean-Pierre Klifa.

His mother, Rolande Klifa, was a respected Paris dressmaker whose studio was filled with bolts of silk, unfinished gowns on mannequins, and women who came not just for clothes, but for confidence. Jean-Pierre watched fittings, heard every conversation about fabric and fit, and learned very early that a beautiful garment can change the way someone walks out the door.

Building a Brand

After business studies in the United States, he chose the harder path: to build something of his own. The JPK brand started with bold, sun-smart resort wear that bridged Parisian elegance with American beach life. The iconic JPK Bucket Bag became a phenomenon — over 200,000 units sold across the country.

The Hilton Head Boutique

Today, the JPK boutique at 1 N Forest Beach Drive is a destination in itself. Vibrant colors, effortless silhouettes, and fabrics designed for the island lifestyle. It's fashion that understands where you are and how you want to feel.

Part of a growing network of 12+ JPK boutiques in premier resort destinations, the Hilton Head location captures everything special about this island — relaxed luxury, bold confidence, and a touch of something you can't quite find anywhere else.
excerpt: How a Parisian fashion house found its American home in a Coligny Plaza boutique.
title: Jean-Pierre Klifa: From Paris to Hilton Head

content:
Twelve miles of broad, hard-packed beach. That's what Hilton Head Island offers, and it's unlike almost anywhere else on the East Coast. The beaches here are wide enough to bike on, firm enough for jogging, and beautiful enough to make you forget wherever you came from.

Coligny Beach

The island's most popular public beach, and for good reason. Easy parking, outdoor showers, restrooms, and a direct connection to the shops and restaurants of Coligny Plaza. Arrive early in summer — this one fills up.

Driessen Beach Park

Quieter than Coligny with excellent facilities. A local favorite for families who want space to spread out without fighting for a spot.

Sea Pines Beach

Inside the gates of Sea Pines Resort, this stretch of beach feels almost private. The iconic red-and-white lighthouse at Harbour Town is a short bike ride away.

Beach Riding & Low Tide

At low tide, Hilton Head's beaches become extraordinary. The hard-packed sand extends hundreds of yards, creating a natural highway for biking, running, or simply walking. Check tide charts and plan accordingly — the best beach experiences happen within two hours of low tide.
excerpt: Twelve miles of pristine Atlantic coastline — here's how to find your perfect stretch of sand.
title: The Ultimate Hilton Head Beach Guide

content:
Hilton Head Island has quietly become one of the Southeast's most exciting culinary destinations. Beyond the expected seafood shacks (though those are wonderful too), the island harbors a collection of restaurants that rival anything you'd find in Charleston or Savannah.

Waterfront Dining

There's something about dining on the water that transforms a meal into an experience. ELA's on the Water at Shelter Cove delivers exactly that — stunning marsh views, impeccable seafood, and a wine list that takes the Lowcountry seriously. Their pan-seared local grouper is reason enough to visit.

European Elegance

Charlie's L'Etoile Verte has been a Hilton Head institution for decades, and with good reason. The French-inspired cuisine uses seasonal, local ingredients in ways that feel both classic and contemporary. The duck confit alone is worth the trip.

Italian Excellence

Michael Anthony's Cucina Italiana brings authentic Italian cooking to the Lowcountry. Hand-crafted pastas, fresh-off-the-boat seafood, and a warmth of service that makes every dinner feel like a celebration.

Iconic Island Dining

The Salty Dog Cafe at South Beach Marina is more than a restaurant — it's a Hilton Head landmark. The sunsets alone are worth the visit, but the fresh seafood and relaxed atmosphere keep visitors and locals coming back year after year.
excerpt: From waterfront seafood to Parisian-inspired fine dining, discover the restaurants that make Hilton Head a culinary destination.
title: The Definitive Guide to Hilton Head's Best Restaurants
